title: The Oxford Dictionary of National Biography
subtitle: In Association with the British Academy
excerpt: The definitive biographical reference for British history, containing over 60,000 detailed biographies of influential figures from every era, compiled by thousands of expert contributors.
keyIdeas: National biography is a form of collective history — the story of a nation told through the lives of its people
The DNB's 60,000+ entries create an unparalleled database of British historical figures
Biography at this scale reveals patterns and connections invisible in individual life stories
keyTakeaways: The DNB covers everyone from kings and prime ministers to obscure inventors and poets
Each entry is written by a specialist scholar and includes a critical assessment of the subject's significance
The Oxford DNB (2004) was a complete revision of the original 1885-1900 Dictionary of National Biography
Entries include a bibliography for further reading
The online edition is updated quarterly with new biographies and corrections
whoShouldRead: Academic researchers needing authoritative biographical information
History enthusiasts wanting to explore British history through biography
Anyone who needs to verify biographical facts about historical British figures
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking biographies of non-British figures
Casual readers who want narrative biographies rather than reference entries
Those without access to a research library or online subscription
